basics.name: Jeff Tong
basics.label: Senior Software Development Engineer
basics.summary: Senior Software Engineer with 15+ years of experience building scalable architectures for analytics, healthcare, and financial/payment platforms. Specializes in fraud analytics, platform modernization, and security-minded product delivery with full-stack development expertise. Passionate about offensive and defensive cybersecurity strategies.

work.name: CVS Health
work.summary: Design, architect, and implement modernization-refactoring of backend services and legacy frontends.
highlights: Refactored legacy MinuteClinic services to FastAPI to improve care provider scheduling experience.

work.name: UglyEgg.AI
highlights: Architected scalable solution for Generative Search Optimization.
Development of frontend with React with feedback from business stakeholders to drive revenue.
Development of analytics and scraping services with Node.js to facilitate core business.

work.name: Starbucks Coffee Company
work.position: Software Development Engineer III - Retail Fraud Analytics
work.summary: Built fraud decisioning, analytics, and internal productivity systems.
highlights: Implemented performance improvements in a custom Spark analytics platform, reducing execution time by about 77%.
Architected and shipped a fraud decisioning and reporting system with REST APIs and Kafka log streaming that increased fraud detection.
Maintained and migrated settlement and reconciliation systems that prevent significant financial loss.
Discovered and reported internal and vendor application vulnerabilities to reduce remote code execution and data breach risk.
Built an internal Retrieval-Augmented Generation assistant on Databricks to improve team resolution time.
Created GitHub Copilot agentic workflows to summarize commits and generate Jira stories for multiple teams.
Prototyped agentic harnesses with Pi for internal orchestration and reporting.

work.name: Starbucks Coffee Company
work.summary: Delivered secure payment platform enhancements for high-volume transaction systems.
highlights: Implemented TOTP (RFC-6238) backend support for the Stars for Everyone launch.
Served as Team Security Champion and influenced secure architecture decisions early in the SDLC.
Supported mission-critical payment services handling millions of dollars in transactions with 24/7 availability.
Delivered Google Pay integration for Payment Service REST APIs used by the mobile app.

work.name: Agilysys
work.summary: Led modernization efforts and microservices adoption for hospitality products.
highlights: Architected modernization from legacy systems to microservices architecture.
Engineered Guest Profile and Itinerary microservices using ASP.NET Core, RabbitMQ, and Docker.
Mentored developers on modern frameworks and Domain-Driven Design.
Led offshore development and QA coordination for distributed delivery.

work.name: BECU
work.summary: Built full-stack applications for lending business lines.
highlights: Delivered full-stack web applications for HELOC and Home Loans lines of business.

work.name: Agilysys
work.summary: Resolved high-impact customer issues and supported major project continuity.
highlights: Provided deep investigations and resolutions for VP/CEO-level customer escalations.
Unblocked projects valued at approximately $2.2M.

work.name: Microsoft
work.summary: Improved engineering throughput and code quality tooling across major product groups.
highlights: Supported the CoreXt build system spanning MSBuild and NMake projects.
Reduced productivity and test-time loss by resolving and preventing build breaks.
Developed a source-control check-in quality tool adopted by Office 365, Xbox Kinect, and Exchange teams.
Contributed work recognized in Engineering Excellence award consideration.

projects.name: Code Chronicles
projects.description: Personal blog focused on InfoSec and software engineering topics.

projects.name: OWASP Wrong Secrets
projects.description: Contributed fix for a challenge issue.
highlights: Issue: https://github.com/OWASP/wrongsecrets/issues/1279

projects.name: Navi
projects.description: Contributed improvements for better Windows support.
highlights: Pull request: https://github.com/denisidoro/navi/pull/1007
